rationale_overall: Fourth consecutive quiet quarter at the cap=8 ceiling: Q4 2025 / Q1 FY26 prints firmed every holding's thesis and no current name suffered a fundamental breach that would justify a forced swap. NVDA's Feb 25 Q4 FY26 report set a record $68.1B revenue with $62.3B data-center on Blackwell ramp and guided Q1 FY27 to $78B; ISRG's Jan 22 Q4 2025 placed 303 da Vinci 5 systems (vs 174 a year ago) on 19% revenue growth and guided 13-15% procedure growth for 2026; LLY's Feb 4 Q4 2025 grew revenue 43% on Mounjaro +110% and Zepbound +122% with orforglipron filed for obesity in US, Japan, and EU, and 2026 revenue guided to $80-83B; LNG's Feb 26 Q4 2025 print delivered $10.68 EPS (146% YoY) with Train 5 first LNG online and Europe importing a record ~125 Mt in 2025; QTUM-tracked names confirmed commercial inflection (IonQ first public quantum co past $100M GAAP revenue, D-Wave bookings +471% QoQ); the nuclear-SMR-hyperscaler buildout continued firming NLR's basket exposure (Microsoft, Amazon, Google all locking firm carbon-free PPAs); MSFT remains the hyperscale-AI anchor via Azure capex and OpenAI partnership. The one real wobble is RKLB's Neutron debut slipping again to NET Q4 2026 (per late-2025 guidance), but the company's 2025 record revenue, growing backlog, and unique rocket exposure mean a slip is not a thesis breach — it costs a quarter, not the holding. The single most attractive external candidate this quarter is AVGO (Mar 4 Q1 FY26: AI revenue +106% YoY to $8.4B, $73B AI backlog, OpenAI added as 6th named custom-silicon customer), but at cap=8 every slot is earning its keep on thesis terms, no current holding shows a breach severe enough to displace, and the disciplined call is to hold the 8 and let the optimizer re-weight rather than churn for the sake of activity. The cap=8 ceiling continues to act as the constraint it was designed to be: forcing every quarter past saturation to clear a strict breach-or-pair bar.
